name: White Chocolate Mousse in an Almond Cookie Shell
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------MOUSSE-----------------------------------
1 cup sugar
1/2 cup water
8 large egg whites
6 large egg yolks
2 tablespoons rum (white)
1 pound white chocolate, melted
Creme fraiche
Raspberry puree
Almond Cookie Shell: ====================
1. In a bowl, beat the egg whites briefly.
2. Add the sugar and flour, then whisk to combine.
3. Stir in the almonds.
4. Butter a baking sheet and spoon tablespoons of mixture onto it.
5. Spread each spoonful slightly with the back of a spoon to form circles about 2 inches apart.
6. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 5-7 minutes. Remove from oven while hot and mold into small cups using a rolling pin. Set aside to dry.
Mousse: =======
1. In a saucepan, heat the sugar and water until they form a soft ball.
2. In a bowl of an electric mixer, beat the egg whites until medium stiff (first on medium speed, then on high).
3. Add the sugar and water from the saucepan to the egg whites and continue beating briefly to form a stiff meringue.
4. In a metal bowl, beat the egg yolks over heat with a whisk.
5. Add rum to the egg yolks while still beating over heat.
6. Fold the egg yolks into the egg whites.
7. Fold in the melted chocolate.
8. Refrigerate for 3-4 hours.
Serve one scoop of mousse in an almond cookie shell. Garnish with raspberry puree and creme fraiche.
